zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Clifton於大約1445年出生在英國藍卡爾時，她的父親John Clifton53歲，母親Joan de Thorpe49歲。她於大約1464年與Thomas III de Clayton在英國藍卡爾克雷頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1470年在她的家鄉享年27歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Clifton于大约1445年出生在英国藍卡爾时，她的父亲John Clifton53岁，母亲Joan de Thorpe49岁。她于大约1464年与Thomas III de Clayton在英国藍卡爾克雷頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1470年在她的家乡享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Clifton was born about 1445, in Lancashire, England, her father, John Clifton, was 53 and her mother, Joan de Thorpe, was 49. She married Thomas III de Clayton about 1464, in Clayton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died about 1470, in her hometown, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Clifton est née environ 1445, à Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, John Clifton, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Joan de Thorpe, avait 49 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas III de Clayton environ 1464, à Clayton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1470, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Clifton um 1445 in Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Clifton 53 und ihre Mutter Joan de Thorpe 49. Sie hat um 1464 in Clayton, Lancashire, England Thomas III de Clayton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb um 1470 im Alter von 27 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Clifton è nata il circa 1445 nel Lancashire, England, sua padre, John Clifton, aveva 53 anni e sua madre, Joan de Thorpe, aveva 49 anni. Lei sposato Thomas III de Clayton circa 1464, nel Clayton, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì circa 1470, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Cliftonが約1445年にイギリスランカシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Cliftonは53歳，母Joan de Thorpeは49歳であった。約1464年にイギリスランカシュアイアクレイトンでThomas III de Claytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1470年に故郷で27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Clifton(이)가 대략 1445년 영국 랭카실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Clifton(은)는 53세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Thorpe(은)는 49세였다. 그녀는 대략 1464년 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴에서 Thomas III de Clayton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1470년 그녀의 고향에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.Abt 1445–Abt 1470: tany ho any amin'ny 1445–tany ho any amin'ny 1470

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Clifton nasceu aproximadamente 1445, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Clifton, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Thorpe, tinha 49 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas III de Clayton aproximadamente 1464, em Clayton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1470, em sua cidade natal, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Clifton родилась около 1445 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Clifton было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Thorpe, 49. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas III de Clayton около 1464 г. в местности клеитон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла около 1470 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 27 лет.

sm.Abt 1445–Abt 1470: e tusa o le 1445–e tusa o le 1470

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Clifton nació aproximadamente 1445, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Clifton, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Joan de Thorpe, tenía 49 años. Se casó con Thomas III de Clayton aproximadamente 1464, en Clayton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1470, en su pueblo, a la edad de 27 años.
